description: Canada's AI governance landscape: AIDA (died in Parliament) and the Voluntary Code of Conduct on Responsible Development and Management of Advanced Generative AI Systems (the only active federal AI-specific instrument).

aida.statusDetail: Bill C-27 died when Parliament prorogued January 6, 2025. Minister confirmed June 2025 that AIDA will not return in original form. Government signaled 'light, tight, right' approach for future AI legislation.

billC27Parts.name: Personal Information and Data Protection Tribunal Act

proposedRequirements: Impact assessments for high-impact AI systems
Criminal offenses for reckless deployment causing serious harm
AI and Data Commissioner for enforcement

voluntaryCode.fullName: Voluntary Code of Conduct on the Responsible Development and Management of Advanced Generative AI Systems
voluntaryCode.issuingBody: Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED)
voluntaryCode.statusDetail: With AIDA dead, this remains the only federal AI-specific governance instrument in Canada, though non-binding.
